name: Pretext
description: Pure JS/TS text measurement & layout engine (no DOM reflow). Uses Canvas measureText + Intl.Segmenter for pixel-accurate multilingual text sizing. Powers masonry grids, virtual scroll, canvas/SVG rendering, shrink-wrap titles, and typographic animations. Source: github.com/chenglou/pretext v0.0.4.
capabilities: measure paragraph height without DOM reflow
layout text into lines with fixed or variable widths
shrink-wrap multiline text to tightest container width
flow text around floated elements (variable-width lines)
CJK, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, emoji, mixed-bidi support
masonry grid layout powered by real text heights
animated typographic reveals and canvas text effects

api_summary.prepare: prepare(text, font, opts?) → PreparedText — one-time segmentation + canvas measurement
api_summary.layout: layout(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) → { height, lineCount } — pure arithmetic, call on resize
api_summary.layoutWithLines: layoutWithLines(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) → { height, lineCount, lines }
api_summary.walkLineRanges: walkLineRanges(prepared, maxWidth, onLine) → number — low-level, no string alloc
api_summary.layoutNextLine: layoutNextLine(prepared, cursor, maxWidth) → LayoutLine | null — variable-width iterator

usage_examples.name: Measure paragraph height (no DOM)
usage_examples.code: const p = prepare('Hello world', '16px Inter'); const { height } = layout(p, 320, 24);

usage_examples.name: Masonry grid
usage_examples.code: items.forEach(item => { const p = prepare(item.text, '15px system-ui'); item.height = layout(p, colWidth, 22).height + PADDING; });

usage_examples.name: Shrink-wrap container width
usage_examples.code: let maxW = 0; walkLineRanges(prepared, 9999, l => { if (l.width > maxW) maxW = l.width; });

usage_examples.name: Text flow around image
usage_examples.code: let cursor = {segmentIndex:0,graphemeIndex:0}; let y=0; while(true){ const w = y < img.bottom ? col - img.width : col; const line = layoutNextLine(p, cursor, w); if(!line) break; ctx.fillText(line.text, 0, y); cursor=line.end; y+=26; }

integration_notes: Bundle is IIFE format — exposes global `Pretext` object with all exported functions.
canvas element must exist in DOM before calling prepare() in browser (canvas.getContext('2d') is used internally).
Call prepare() once per text+font combination. Only re-call if text or font changes.
Call layout() / layoutWithLines() on every resize — it is ~0.09ms per call.
Server-side usage is experimental — canvas polyfill (e.g. node-canvas) required.
system-ui font gives inaccurate results on macOS. Use named fonts (Inter, Georgia, etc.).
